entry.title: Writing Skills | Xkcd
entry.content: Another gem from xkcd.

entry.title: My LA Boogie Nights Bar | Messy Nessy Chic
entry.content:
After 24 hours in the city of Angels, I had yet to find the perfect LA hangout I was looking for. I have this romantic vision of Angelinos hanging out on seventies lawn chairs in their backyards with twinkle lights hanging from the trees and margaritas in hand. I love it when my imagination becomes a reality…

It certainly looks like a fun place.

entry.title: Posters From the Spanish Civil War | Retronaut
entry.content:
For obvious reasons, I find this fascinating.

Via Coudal Partners.

entry.title: Brent Simmons on Social Media
entry.content:
My blog’s older than Twitter and Facebook, and it will outlive them. It has seen Flickr explode and then fade. It’s seen Google Wave and Google Reader come and go, and it’ll still be here as Google Plus fades. When Medium and Tumblr are gone, my blog will be here. The things that will last on the internet are not owned. Plain old websites, blogs, RSS, irc, email.

I couldn’t agree more. It doesn’t matter how big a certain social platforms may be today, its long-term survival is far from certain. If you want your work to endure the test of time, you must publish it on your own platform.

entry.title: Postcards From Stockholm on Vimeo
entry.content:
Play it on full-screen mode. What a gorgeous city.

Via Coudal Partners.
